_meta.description: Maps freezable-token contracts to the issuer that controls them. When a freeze target's holdings include one of these tokens, the brief generator knows who to address. All addresses lowercased.

schema_changes.v2: Added `delegates_to` field for wrapper tokens (Aave aTokens) whose freeze action chains through to an underlying issuer.

_meta.source_caveat: These contact emails and freeze powers are based on public knowledge as of 2025-2026. Verify before sending. Issuer policies change.

tokens.freeze_notes: DAI is permissionless at the contract level — there is no individual-address freeze function in the DAI ERC-20 contract. Sky governance can pause specific protocol functions (e.g., Spark lending) but cannot blacklist holders. Practical recovery path for DAI seizure: identify the perpetrator and pursue via court order, NOT via issuer freeze. Surface Sky governance as a courtesy contact for visibility only.
Circle has demonstrated freeze capability via blacklist on multiple occasions. Compliance request through their LE portal.
Tether has frozen billions in USDT and is generally responsive to law enforcement requests. Submit through their LE channel.
Paxos retains freeze capability on remaining BUSD supply. Note: BUSD was deprecated by NYDFS — minting halted.
Paxos issues PYUSD on PayPal's behalf and has freeze capability.
Midas issues mSyrup* tokens via permissioned mint/redeem and can freeze specific holders. EU-regulated (BaFin).
rETH is a fully decentralized liquid staking token — no freeze capability exists.
stETH is a permissionless liquid staking token. Lido DAO has no individual-address freeze capability.
Coinbase issues cbBTC and retains freeze capability via their compliance program.
tBTC is a decentralized BTC bridge token — no freeze capability exists at the contract level.
Native Arbitrum USDC issued by Circle. Same compliance channel as Ethereum USDC.
Arbitrum-native USDT issued by Tether.
Solana USDC issued by Circle.
Solana USDT issued by Tether.

tokens._section: v0.7.5 additions — Maple companion products, Frax, Aave aTokens, BTC wrappers, additional regulated stables

tokens.freeze_notes: Maple's syrupUSDC vault token. Maple's permissioned-pool design and KYC/AML compliance allows them to restrict redemptions for specific holders. Submit freeze requests via their compliance contact with the wallet address and case context.
Maple's syrupUSDT vault token. Same compliance channel as syrupUSDC.

FRAX is partially algorithmic with a governance-controlled component. Frax governance can blacklist specific addresses via a vote, but the process is slow and requires demonstrated cause. Worth contacting on substantial cases; not a fast freeze action.
Staked FRAX vault token. Same governance constraints as FRAX itself.

tokens._section: Aave aTokens — delegate freeze to underlying issuer. When a perpetrator holds an aToken, the freeze action against the UNDERLYING is what actually constrains the position; on aToken redemption the user receives the (now-frozen) underlying token.

tokens.freeze_notes: aUSDC is an Aave receipt token; Aave protocol has no individual freeze capability at the token level. However, the underlying USDC IS freezable through Circle (see delegates_to). The recommended freeze action chains through to the underlying — when Circle freezes USDC at the aToken contract address, redemptions return frozen USDC.
aUSDT receipt token. Delegates to USDT issuer (Tether) for freeze action.

aDAI receipt token. DAI itself is permissionless so the underlying delegate has no freeze either — recovery for aDAI requires perpetrator identification + court order, like raw DAI. Surfaced for completeness.

BitGo custodies the underlying BTC backing WBTC. While the WBTC ERC-20 contract is permissionless, BitGo can refuse redemptions and (in coordination with merchants) blacklist addresses. Practical recovery path: contact BitGo + the merchant network with a court order.

WETH is a canonical wrapper contract for ETH (deposit ETH, mint WETH 1:1; redeem WETH for ETH 1:1). No individual freeze capability. Holdings reported as WETH should be treated as raw ETH for seizure purposes — perpetrator-identification + court order is the path.
Pax Dollar (formerly PAX). Paxos retains freeze capability under their NYDFS license. Submit through their compliance channel.

TrueUSD; ownership transferred from TrustToken to Techteryx in 2020. Freeze capability via the contract's blacklist function. Note: TUSD has had documented reserve transparency issues; freezes may be slower than Circle/Tether.
First Digital USD; issued by First Digital Trust under a Hong Kong trust company license. Freeze capability via blacklist; compliance generally responsive to court-order-backed requests.
